elixir-lang/elixir#14424.body:
### Elixir and Erlang/OTP versions

Erlang/OTP 27 [erts-15.1.2] [source] [64-bit] [smp:8:8] [ds:8:8:10] [async-threads:1] [jit:ns]

Elixir 1.18.2 (compiled with Erlang/OTP 27)

### Operating system

arch & ubuntu

### Current behavior

I'm not sure if this actually belongs here but it felt more misplaced putting it into phoenixframework repo, so sorry in advance.
Today after digging through some coverage files by random we found couple of false negatives which are definitely tested and assumed that just be the size of the project it somehow got swallowed but I still wanted to reproduce it and did a new project.

```bash
mix phx.new foo
mix phx.gen.context Accounts User users name:string
mix test --cover
open cover/Elixir.Foo.Accounts.html
```

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d1d634b0-1efd-4c0f-80de-f20aff4ece25)

I reproduced the behavior on my work ubuntu and now my arch machine and in all cases the `create_user/1` is not covered.


### Expected behavior

This line should be covered.
elixir-lang/elixir#14424.title: Coverage issue and possible false negative

phoenixframework/phoenix#5791.body:
We can't use guards with `assert_push` and al., e.g.:

```elixir
assert_push "presence_state", state when state == %{}
```

### Environment

* Elixir version (elixir -v): Erlang/OTP 26 [erts-14.2.4] [source] [64-bit] [smp:8:8] [ds:8:8:10] [async-threads:1] [jit]
* Phoenix version (mix deps): 1.7.12
* Operating system:

### Actual behavior

```
    error: cannot invoke remote function :erlang.==/2 inside a match
    │
 49 │     assert_push "presence_state", state when state == %{}
    │                                                    ^
    │
    └─ test/enaia_web/channels/online_users_channel_test.exs:49:52: EnaiaWeb.OnlineUsersChannelTest.assert_state_push/1
```

### Expected behavior

It would ideally produce code equivalent to
```elixir
    assert_receive %Phoenix.Socket.Message{
                     event: "presence_state",
                     payload: state
                   }
                   when state == %{}
```
phoenixframework/phoenix#5791.title: Support guards in assert_push

phoenixframework/phoenix#6154.body:
### Environment

* Elixir version (elixir -v): 1.18.3
* Phoenix version (mix deps): latest `main` ([`c73c1b0`](https://github.com/phoenixframework/phoenix/commit/c73c1b0b4ca3fd0adf57bb2010d097d47d8f0768))
* Operating system: macOS

### Actual behavior

<!--
Describe the actual behaviour. If you are seeing an error, include the full message and stacktrace.

If possible, also provide a single file app that reproduces the behaviour, you can start here:
https://github.com/wojtekmach/mix_install_examples/blob/main/phoenix.exs
-->

I followed the [instructions from the README](https://github.com/phoenixframework/phoenix?tab=readme-ov-file#generating-a-phoenix-project-from-unreleased-versions) to create a Phoenix project from an unreleased version.

Executing the command `mix phx.new dev_app --dev`  in the `/installer` directory results in

```
== Compilation error in file lib/phx_new/web.ex ==
** (File.Error) could not read file "[...]/phoenix/installer/templates/phx_web/components/core_components.ex": no such file or directory
```

In https://github.com/phoenixframework/phoenix/pull/6126 the `core_components.ex` file was removed from the `/installer` directory. So copying it from `/priv/templates/phx.gen.live/core_components.ex` to `/installer/templates/phx_web/components/core_components.ex` locally resolves the issue.

### Expected behavior

I expect an application to be generated by following the instructions from the README.

We might want to update the instructions based on the removal of the `core_component.ex` file in https://github.com/phoenixframework/phoenix/pull/6126
phoenixframework/phoenix#6154.title: Missing `core_components.ex` when generating Phoenix project from unreleased version

phoenixframework/phoenix#6172.body:
### Environment

* Elixir version (elixir -v): 1.18.1
* Phoenix version (mix deps): 1.8.0-rc.0
* Operating system: Linux/Ubuntu 22.04

### Actual behavior

I am building the Hello app in the 1.8.0-rc.0 contexts guide. When adding a button component to the 
ProductController.show function:

```heex
<.button href={~p"/cart_items?product_id=#{@product.id}"} method="post">
```

The following warning appears on compilation:

```
    warning: undefined attribute "method" for component HelloWeb.CoreComponents.button/1
    │
 12 │       <.button href={~p"/cart_items?product_id=#{@product.id}"} method="post">
    │       ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
    │
    └─ lib/hello_web/controllers/product_html/show.html.heex:12: (file)
```

### Expected behavior

The application should compile without warning.

In `core_components.ex`,  `:method` should be added to the global attributes:

```
--- a/lib/hello_web/components/core_components.ex
+++ b/lib/hello_web/components/core_components.ex
@@ -88,7 +88,7 @@ defmodule HelloWeb.CoreComponents do
       <.button phx-click="go" variant="primary">Send!</.button>
       <.button navigate={~p"/"}>Home</.button>
   """
-  attr :rest, :global, include: ~w(href navigate patch)
+  attr :rest, :global, include: ~w(href navigate patch method)
   attr :variant, :string, values: ~w(primary)
   slot :inner_block, required: true
```
phoenixframework/phoenix#6172.title: Missing :method attribute in button core component

phoenixframework/phoenix#6188.body:
:wave: When using the default suggested styles or part there of, the nature of the `data-phx-root-id` attribute means that the page is rendered before it is applied, meaning the behaviour of the container changes from its default to `contents` over the course of the page mount lifecycle, which is avoided if we either render the attribute in the static pass, or change which attribute is used.

### Environment

* Elixir version (elixir -v): 1.18.3
* Phoenix version (mix deps): From git main / b1c459943b3279f97725787b9150ff4950958d12
* Operating system: MacOS

### Actual behavior

`data-phx-root-id` is applied either by the LiveSocket JS or by the connected mount, changing the container div to `display: contents;`

### Expected behavior

That the `display: contents;` rule is applied consistently despite the state of the liveview / JS.

### Proposed fixes

That the static render includes the attribute even if empty, or the default css uses something like `data-phx-main` to apply it.
phoenixframework/phoenix#6188.title: Liveview container div exhibits "flash of unstyled content" like behaviour.

phoenixframework/phoenix#6192.body:
stroke-width=1.5 makes for very wispy icons, and the solid ones, such as in the flash area are essentially indistinguishable from each other.

Despite some commentary in the heroicons github, it does not appear that the stroke-width is adjustible (and further does not work for the solid icons, as pictured below). 

This image is from a phoenix app:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/70800b59-898a-46d8-83f9-d757cf8f4e6a)

This image is from the heroicons homepage:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/44b60ee4-c98a-44fa-a86a-4d8981a8e0bd)

This is a pretty significant accessibility problem. I don't know if there's a good way to fix it short of switching vendors.  Is this configurable?
phoenixframework/phoenix#6192.title: Tailwind heroicons are effectively illegible at reasonable screen DPI.

phoenixframework/phoenix#6203.body:
👋 

### Environment

* Elixir version (elixir -v): 1.18.2
* Phoenix version (mix deps): 1.7.14
* Operating system:

### Actual behavior

It seems like a static digested `site.webmanifest` is not being served by Plug.Static.

```elixir
def static_paths, do: ~w(assets fonts images favicon.ico robots.txt site.webmanifest)
```

```elixir
iex> File.ls("lib/bend-0.1.0/priv/static")
#==> {:ok,
#==>  [..., "site-c0c4f1c0f3ba8cb5034fdd9d1aafa9aa.webmanifest",
#==>   "favicon-91f37b602a111216f1eef3aa337ad763.ico", "site.webmanifest",
#==>   "favicon-07f80321aa54b0c7f90564a833181023.ico"]}
```

the Plug.Static `init`ed opts are

```elixir
%{
  at: [],
  from: {:my_app, "priv/static"},
  headers: %{},
  only_rules: {["assets", "fonts", "images", "favicon.ico", "robots.txt",
    "site.webmanifest"], []},
  encodings: [{"gzip", ".gz"}],
  content_types: %{},
  et_cache: "public",
  et_generation: nil,
  qs_cache: "public, max-age=31536000, immutable"
}
```

and the requested path for site.webmanifest is

```
10:22:46.417 request_id=GDdik4hrQd68HZoAGg-D [info] GET /site-c0c4f1c0f3ba8cb5034fdd9d1aafa9aa.webmanifest
10:22:46.419 request_id=GDdik4hrQd68HZoAGg-D [info] Sent 404 in 1ms
```

I think the problem might also affect (digested) favicons and robots.txt but I haven't checked.

### Expected behavior

All digested files get served :)
phoenixframework/phoenix#6203.title: static digested files without prefix get 404

phoenixframework/phoenix#6204.body:
### Environment * Elixir version (elixir -v): 1.18.3 * Phoenix version (mix deps): latest main * Operating system: macOS ### Actual behavior I noticed that daisyUI recently deprecated the `fieldset-label` class, which is being used inside the `core_components.ex`. See https://github.com/saadeghi/daisyui/blob/master/packages/daisyui/src/components/fieldset.css#L11 Also, I think the *label* of the `input` component should be wrapped in a legend HTML tag, as it is used more as a title for the fieldset than as a label. This is how it is done in the daisyUI docs, too (see [daisyUI `input` component](https://daisyui.com/components/input/) for example). Although the `legend` tag is technically optional, it should be used for a11y reasons. > The first element inside the fieldset must be a legend element, which provides a label or description for the group. See [H71: Providing a description for groups of form controls using fieldset and legend elements](https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG-TECHS/H71.html) That's why I suggest refactoring the `input` component to something like this: ```diff def input(assigns) do ~H"""
- + {@label} + <.error :for={msg <- @errors}>{msg}
""" ``` Still, the `legend` tag would only be used if a `label` is provided. I am not sure if we want to require the `label` attribute, but in terms of a11y we have to do it 🤔 The other components like `type: "select"` and `type: "textarea"` would need to be factored, too.
phoenixframework/phoenix#6204.title: CoreComponents use deprecated daisyUI `fieldset-label` class and missing a `legend` tag

phoenixframework/phoenix#6217.body:
Hi,

I've been going through the contexts/data modeling guide and I believe that it is missing some emphasis on being careful when using changesets with internal AND external data.

One thing that stood out to me is that the final steps in completing the CartController are potentially letting a bug slip through where a user can modify the price of a product when adding it to their cart.

How can that happen?
1. The CartController takes in any cart_params
https://github.com/phoenixframework/phoenix/blob/37c6085414daefdc88a4a303c876a616d425a4d7/guides/data_modelling/cross_context_boundaries.md?plain=1#L515-L527

2. It passes them to the update cart function on the context
https://github.com/phoenixframework/phoenix/blob/37c6085414daefdc88a4a303c876a616d425a4d7/guides/data_modelling/cross_context_boundaries.md?plain=1#L533-L557

3. The function then casts the `:items` associations from the cart_params using the cart item's changeset
https://github.com/phoenixframework/phoenix/blob/37c6085414daefdc88a4a303c876a616d425a4d7/guides/data_modelling/cross_context_boundaries.md?plain=1#L540

4. The changeset function casts the `:price_when_carted` attribute
https://github.com/phoenixframework/phoenix/blob/37c6085414daefdc88a4a303c876a616d425a4d7/guides/data_modelling/cross_context_boundaries.md?plain=1#L181-L201

5. Therefore, the user can modify the price of an item in their cart by including the `price_when_carted` property in the form with a request that looks roughly like this
```sh
http -f PUT :4000/cart 'cart[items][0][id]=1' 'cart[items][0][quantity]=5' 'cart[items][0][price_when_carted]=0.01'
```
phoenixframework/phoenix#6217.title: Data modeling guide suggests code that can be exploited

phoenixframework/phoenix#6219.body:
---

### Environment

- **Elixir version**: 1.18.3 (compiled with Erlang/OTP 27)  
- **Phoenix version**: 1.8.0-rc.1  
- **Operating system**: Linux hs-lws 6.12.21-4-MANJARO  x86_64 GNU/Linux

---

### Actual Behavior

When using generators inside an umbrella project, scoped fields (like `user_id`) are not being created as expected.

Steps to reproduce:

```bash
mix phx.new myapp --umbrella
cd myapp/apps/my_app_web
mix phx.gen.auth ...
mix phx.gen.live UserServices UserService user_services service_id:references:services start_date:date end_date:date --prefix info
```

Generated migration:

```elixir
defmodule MyApp.Repo.Migrations.CreateUserServices do
  use Ecto.Migration

  def change do
    create table(:user_services, prefix: :info) do
      add :start_date, :date
      add :end_date, :date

      add :service_id, references(:services, on_delete: :nothing)
      timestamps()
    end

    create index(:user_services, [:service_id])
  end
end
```

---

### Expected Behavior

I expected the generator to include a default scoped reference like `user_id` in the migration, similar to how it behaves in non-umbrella apps:

```elixir
defmodule MyApp.Repo.Migrations.CreateUserServices do
  use Ecto.Migration

  def change do
    create table(:user_services, prefix: :info) do
      add :start_date, :date
      add :end_date, :date

      add :service_id, references(:services, on_delete: :nothing)
      add :user_id, references(:users, on_delete: :nothing)
      timestamps()
    end

    create index(:user_services, [:service_id])
    create index(:user_services, [:user_id])
  end
end
```

---
phoenixframework/phoenix#6219.title: Generators omit default scope fields inside umbrella projects

phoenixframework/phoenix#6226.body:
### Environment * Elixir version (elixir -v): 1.18.3 * Phoenix version (mix deps): 1.8.0-rc.2 * Operating system: NixOS ### Actual behavior When calling `phx.gen.auth` multiple times within the same application, the default implementation will cause errors and test failures because it will always set an assign called `@current_scope`. The generated code looks like ```html
    <%= if @current_scope do %>
  • {@current_scope.admin.email}
  • <.link href={~p"/admins/settings"}>Settings
  • <.link href={~p"/admins/log-out"} method="delete">Log out
    • <% else %>
  • <.link href={~p"/admins/register"}>Register
  • <.link href={~p"/admins/log-in"}>Log in
    • <% end %>
    <%= if @current_scope do %>
  • {@current_scope.user.email}
  • <.link href={~p"/users/settings"}>Settings
  • <.link href={~p"/users/log-out"} method="delete">Log out
    • <% else %>
  • <.link href={~p"/users/register"}>Register
  • <.link href={~p"/users/log-in"}>Log in
    • <% end %>
``` Note how the check is `if @current_scope`, but then inside it will check for either `@current_scope.admin` or `@current_scope.user`. You will always enter both of the if expressions if there is a current scope assigned. Once we are inside of the if, we will always get an error from one of them. ### Expected behavior I would expect a brand new phoenix application that has only called the generators and no other code changes would pass the tests that it generates and cause no errors. I think it would be ideal to instead call these scope assigns something like `@current_user_scope` and `@current_admin_scope`. I believe it could be beneficial to have both be valid and available at the same time. Similar to how it worked previously where we had `@current_user` and `@current_admin` assigns.
phoenixframework/phoenix#6226.title: `phx.gen.auth` breaks because of `current_scope` when called multiple times

phoenixframework/phoenix#6229.body:
Today we have a checkbox for "keep logged in" but I suggest to have two buttons instead, both for confirmation and authentication. In both cases, the first button is a primary button.

## Confirmation

* Confirm and keep me logged in
* Confirm and log me in only this time

## Log in

* Keep me logged in on this device
* Log me on only this time

For the sudo mode, we can either show a single button... or we can show the same buttons as "Log in", perhaps allowing us to simplify some of the logic related passing remember me cookies around.
phoenixframework/phoenix#6229.title: Improve authentication/confirmation flow

phoenixframework/phoenix#6241.body:
Hi,

There is some misalignment in the documentation for socket authentication in the channels guide and in the generated `socket.js` file from `phx.gen.socket`.

The guide suggests using the transport agnostic method of sending the user token through a header, while the file suggests using the older approach of sending it as a query parameter.

https://github.com/phoenixframework/phoenix/blob/69e2cfa13b94e75bb749bd912f3727e9e4fd410c/guides/real_time/channels.md?plain=1#L453-L457

https://github.com/phoenixframework/phoenix/blob/2875c54cddec2fc45258baee9a84cc8193cad541/priv/templates/phx.gen.socket/socket.js#L7-L9
phoenixframework/phoenix#6241.title: Misalignment in socket authentication documentation

phoenixframework/phoenix#6251.body:
I've just taken 1.8.0-rc3 for a spin and it's looking really great 🎉 .  I noticed that a few dependencies were out of date in the newly created project so wanted to ask if there was any appetite to update them so new projects start out with the latest deps.

Happy to submit PRs for these, just wanted to check if they were desired.

- daisyUI v5.0.8 < v5.0.35 [assets/vendor]
- tailwindcss v4.0.9 < v4.1.7 [config/config.exs]
- esbuild v0.17.11 < v0.25.4 [config/config.exs]
- dns_cluster v0.1.3 < 0.2.0 [mix.exs]
- heroicons v2.1.1 < v2.2.0 [mix.exs]

I've tested them all except dns_cluster v0.2.0 (though I am using that in other projects successfully).
phoenixframework/phoenix#6251.title: [1.8.0-rc3] New project outdated dependencies

phoenixframework/phoenix#6252.body:
I'm still wrapping my head around scopes, but AFAIU while the only way to currently generate a scope is via `phx.gen.auth` they are intended to be used without auth also. Put another way, even if an app doesn't require auth, developers are still encouraged to leverage scopes.

Further, it seems that the `test_login_helper` value basically gets inserted as a `setup` callback in tests for resources that were generated within that scope (i.e., `setup :register_and_log_in_user` in tests, if `test_login_helper: :register_and_log_in_user` is in config.exs).

But what if we want to use a **scope that isn't related to auth**? For argument's sake, let's say `GeoRestriction`. If we generate a new resource within this scope, it would make sense that generated tests call a setup callback to ensure a `GeoRestriction` instance is present in scope. But why should the attribute for that be `test_login_helper` when there's no logging in taking place (in tests or the app itself)? Wouldn't it be more appropriate to rename the attribute to something that isn't coupled to auth and login, like simply `test_helper`?
phoenixframework/phoenix#6252.title: [1.8.0-rc3] `test_login_helper` scope config param -> rename to decouple from auth?

phoenixframework/phoenix#6287.body:
### Environment

* Elixir version (elixir -v): Elixir 1.18.3
* Phoenix version (mix deps): 1.7.21
* Operating system: Mac

### Actual behavior

Running `mix phx.gen.presence` from within an umbrella app `apps/hello_web` generates this:

```ex
defmodule HelloWeb.Presence do
  use Phoenix.Presence,
    otp_app: :hello_web,
    pubsub_server: HelloWeb.PubSub # <-- This is wrong PubSub
end
```

### Expected behavior

It should use the correct PubSub from main app:

```ex
defmodule HelloWeb.Presence do
  use Phoenix.Presence,
    otp_app: :hello_web,
    pubsub_server: Hello.PubSub
end
```
phoenixframework/phoenix#6287.title: Running `mix phx.gen.presence` in umbrella app uses wrong PubSub.

phoenixframework/phoenix#6319.body:
### Environment

* Elixir version (elixir -v): 1.18.4
* Erlang version: 28.0.1
* Phoenix version (mix deps): 1.8.0-rc.3
* Operating system: macOs, Ubuntu

### Actual behavior

`mix phx.gen.cert` results in the following in a newly created project:

```
** (MatchError) no match of right hand side value: {:error, {:asn1, {{:case_clause, :NULL}, [{:"OTP-PKIX", :"-getenc_internal_object_set_argument_2/1-fun-11-", 3, [file: ~c"../src/OTP-PKIX.erl", line: 2419]}, {:"OTP-PKIX", :enc_OTPTBSCertificate_signature, 2, [file: ~c"../src/OTP-PKIX.erl", line: 492]}, {:"OTP-PKIX", :enc_OTPTBSCertificate, 2, [file: ~c"../src/OTP-PKIX.erl", line: 418]}, {:"OTP-PKIX", :encode, 2, [file: ~c"../src/OTP-PKIX.erl", line: 134]}, {:public_key, :pkix_encode, 3, [file: ~c"public_key.erl", line: 910]}, {:public_key, :pkix_sign, 2, [file: ~c"public_key.erl", line: 1461]}, {Mix.Tasks.Phx.Gen.Cert, :certificate_and_key, 3, [file: ~c"lib/mix/tasks/phx.gen.cert.ex", line: 113]}, {Mix.Tasks.Phx.Gen.Cert, :run, 1, [file: ~c"lib/mix/tasks/phx.gen.cert.ex", line: 73]}]}}}
    (public_key 1.18.1) public_key.erl:914: :public_key.pkix_encode/3
    (public_key 1.18.1) public_key.erl:1461: :public_key.pkix_sign/2
    (phoenix 1.8.0-rc.3) lib/mix/tasks/phx.gen.cert.ex:113: Mix.Tasks.Phx.Gen.Cert.certificate_and_key/3
    (phoenix 1.8.0-rc.3) lib/mix/tasks/phx.gen.cert.ex:73: Mix.Tasks.Phx.Gen.Cert.run/1
    (mix 1.18.4) lib/mix/task.ex:495: anonymous fn/3 in Mix.Task.run_task/5
    (mix 1.18.4) lib/mix/cli.ex:107: Mix.CLI.run_task/2
```

Here is a project for reproduction: https://github.com/krns/phx_gen_cert_bug

### Expected behavior

The cert should be generated.
phoenixframework/phoenix#6319.title: mix phx.gen.cert is not working on newly created project

phoenixframework/phoenix#6377.body:
### Environment

* Elixir version (elixir -v):
Erlang/OTP 27 [erts-15.2.7] [source] [64-bit] [smp:32:32] [ds:32:32:10] [async-threads:1] [jit:ns]
Elixir 1.18.4 (compiled with Erlang/OTP 25)

* Phoenix version (mix deps): 1.8.0-rc.4

* Operating system: Linux Mint 22

### Actual behavior

Running this in the terminal:

```
mix phx.gen.context Organizations Member members user_id:references:users role:string
```

The generated migration and schema are wrong.

```
defmodule Stasis.Repo.Migrations.CreateMembers do
  use Ecto.Migration

  def change do
    create table(:members, primary_key: false) do
      add :id, :binary_id, primary_key: true
      add :role, :string
      add :user_id, references(:users, on_delete: :nothing, type: :binary_id)
      add :user_id, references(:users, type: :binary_id, on_delete: :delete_all)

      timestamps(type: :utc_datetime)
    end

    create index(:members, [:user_id])

    create index(:members, [:user_id])
  end
end

defmodule Stasis.Organizations.Member do
  use Ecto.Schema
  import Ecto.Changeset

  @primary_key {:id, :binary_id, autogenerate: true}
  @foreign_key_type :binary_id
  schema "members" do
    field :role, :string
    field :user_id, :binary_id
    field :user_id, :binary_id

    timestamps(type: :utc_datetime)
  end

  @doc false
  def changeset(member, attrs, user_scope) do
    member
    |> cast(attrs, [:role])
    |> validate_required([:role])
    |> put_change(:user_id, user_scope.user.id)
  end
end
```

The user_id is added twice in both. And differently in the migration.

### Expected behavior

I would expect the schema to have this field:

```
belongs_to :user, Stasis.Accounts.User

# or at least just one:

field :user_id, :binary_id
```

And the migration to be:

```
add :user_id, references(:users, type: :binary_id, on_delete: :delete_all)
```
phoenixframework/phoenix#6377.title: mix phx.gen.context with references field generates the field and migration incorrectly.

phoenixframework/phoenix#6391.body:
### Environment

* Elixir version (elixir -v): 1.16.3
* Phoenix version (mix deps): 1.8.0-rc.4
* Operating system: macOS

### Actual behavior

The [recently-introduced tsconfig.json](https://github.com/phoenixframework/phoenix/pull/6268/files#diff-ebb59150eb910be95f490a5b4c9d5d6b2ccc62c7137817506d3f6b9beba507d9R5) file seems to be breaking node module resolution. [This was discovered](https://github.com/inertiajs/inertia-phoenix/issues/59) when attempting to use the `@inertiajs/react` package in a clean Phoenix installation.

Here's an example repository: https://github.com/derrickreimer/phx-tsconfig-paths-repro

```
git checkout https://github.com/derrickreimer/phx-tsconfig-paths-repro.git
cd phx-tsconfig-paths-repro
cd assets && npm install
cd ../
mix setup
```

When running `mix assets.build`, the following warning appears in the output:

```
▲ [WARNING] Import "createInertiaApp" will always be undefined because the file "node_modules/@inertiajs/react/dist/index.js" has no exports [import-is-undefined]

    js/app.jsx:4:9:
      4 │ import { createInertiaApp } from "@inertiajs/react";
        ╵          ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
```

Consequently, the built JavaScript errors when executed on the client:

```
09:19:04.274 Uncaught ReferenceError: module is not defined
    <anonymous> index.ts:1
index.ts:1:1
```

### Expected behavior

The build should succeed. Removing the `paths` override from the tsconfig.json file fixes the build. Though this override appears to be technically correct (since it still includes `node_modules` in the places to resolve), it breaks esbuild nonetheless.

Rather than overriding global path resolution (`"*"`), perhaps it would be safer to create phoenix-specific path alias like `"phx_deps/*"`?

```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "baseUrl": ".",
    "paths": {
      "phx_deps/*": ["../deps/*"]
    },
    "allowJs": true,
    "noEmit": true
  },
  "include": ["js/**/*"]
}
```
phoenixframework/phoenix#6391.title: Overriding paths in tsconfig.json breaks Node module resolution

phoenixframework/phoenix#6400.body:
### Environment

* Elixir version (elixir -v): 1.8
* Phoenix version (mix deps): latest
* Operating system: -

### Actual behavior
The usage rule for liveview (usage-rules/liveview.md) states:

“Never write embedded <script> tags in HEEx. Instead always write your scripts and hooks in the assets/js directory and integrate them with the assets/js/app.js file”

### Expected behavior

“Write JavaScript hooks either in the assets/js directory and integrate them with assets/js/app.js, or use colocated hooks with <script :type={Phoenix.LiveView.ColocatedHook} name=".HookName"> directly in HEEx templates (Phoenix LiveView 1.1+). Never use regular <script> tags without the colocated hook attributes.”
phoenixframework/phoenix#6400.title: Update usage_rule for script tags in heex templates with colocated hook usage

phoenixframework/phoenix#6405.body:
### Environment

* Elixir version (elixir -v): 1.18.4
* Phoenix version (mix deps): 1.8
* Operating system: MacOS 15.6

### Actual behavior
When running `mix phx.gen.live` after having ran `mix phx.gen.auth Accounts User users`, there is a `subscribe_*/1` and `broadcast/2` functions added to the created context. For example, `mix phx.gen.live Animals Cat cats name:string` would create the following two functions in the `Animals` Context:
```elixir
def subscribe_cats(%Scope{} = scope) do
  key = scope.user.id

  Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(MyApp.PubSub, "user:#{key}:cats")
end

defp broadcast(%Scope{} = scope, message) do
  key = scope.user.id

  Phoenix.PubSub.broadcast(MyApp.PubSub, "user:#{key}:cats", message)
end
```

If you then ran `mix phx.gen.live Animals Dog dogs name:string`, the following function would get added to the `Animals` Context:
```elixir
def subscribe_dogs(%Scope{} = scope) do
  key = scope.user.id

  Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(MyApp.PubSub, "user:#{key}:dogs")
end
```

However, the `broadcast/2` function remains untouched only broadcasting to `"user:#{key}:cats"`:
```elixir
defp broadcast(%Scope{} = scope, message) do
  key = scope.user.id

  Phoenix.PubSub.broadcast(MyApp.PubSub, "user:#{key}:cats", message)
end
```

If before running the dogs generator you renamed `broadcast/2` to `broadcast_cats/2`:
```elixir
defp broadcast_cats(%Scope{} = scope, message) do
  key = scope.user.id

  Phoenix.PubSub.broadcast(MyApp.PubSub, "user:#{key}:cats", message)
end
```

Then you run `mix phx.gen.live Animals Dog dogs name:string`. A new `broadcast/2` function is not created and the dog related functions, like the one below, still call `broadcast/2` which causes compile time errors since it no longer exists.
```elixir
def create_dog(%Scope{} = scope, attrs) do
  with {:ok, dog = %Dog{}} <-
         %Dog{}
         |> Dog.changeset(attrs, scope)
         |> Repo.insert() do
    broadcast(scope, {:created, dog})
    {:ok, dog}
  end
end
```

### Expected behavior
I would expect either separate `subscribe_*/1` and `broadcast_*/2` functions for each resource, like `subscribe_cats`, `subscribe_dogs`, `broadcast_cats`, `broadcast_dogs`, etc, or to have the functions changed in a way where there's a single `subscribe` and `broadcast` function for all resources.
phoenixframework/phoenix#6405.title: `phx.gen.live` creates a `broadcast/2` function in the Context which can't handle more than one resource

phoenixframework/phoenix#6406.body:
### Environment

* Elixir version (elixir -v): Elixir 1.18.4 (compiled with Erlang/OTP 27)
* Phoenix version (mix deps): 1.8.0
* Operating system: Ubuntu 22.04

### Actual behavior
running mix phx.gen.auth etc injects extra information into AGENTS.md including the lines
```
- **Always** be mindful of where to place routes. `phx.gen.auth` creates multiple router plugs and `live_session` scopes:
  - A plug `:fetch_current_user` that is included in the default browser pipeline
  
```

### Expected behavior
```
- **Always** be mindful of where to place routes. `phx.gen.auth` creates multiple router plugs and `live_session` scopes:
  - A plug `:fetch_current_scope_for_user` that is included in the default browser pipeline
  
```
There might be other text in that section which needs to be updated for the new scopes in 1.8
phoenixframework/phoenix#6406.title: phx.gen.auth injects incorrect text into AGENTS.md

phoenixframework/phoenix#6442.body:
This PR splits the command from it's output in the docs to fix #6441 

Feel free to ignore if you prefer to keep it the way it is ... 👌 
I'm sure people can figure it out for themselves, just feels like a unpleasant experience IMO. 🙃
phoenixframework/phoenix#6442.title: PR: Split the Generator Command from Output for Clarity Fixes #6441

phoenixframework/phoenix#6524.body:
### Environment * Elixir version (elixir -v): 1.19.1 * Phoenix version (mix deps): 1.8.1 * Operating system: macOS 26 ### Actual behavior With a route containing a unicode character, the `~p` sigil will warn that the route does not exist. ```elixir # in lib/unicode_verified_routes_web/router.ex get("/ø", HomeController, :index) # in eg. a template Ø ``` ``` $ mix compile warning: no route path for UnicodeVerifiedRoutesWeb.Router matches "/ø" │ 1 │ Ø │ ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ │ └─ lib/unicode_verified_routes_web/controllers/page_html/home.html.heex:1: UnicodeVerifiedRoutesWeb.PageHTML.home/1 ```
Reproduction code. However the compile warning is not shown when running as .exs ```elixir Application.put_env(:phoenix, :json_library, JSON) Application.put_env(:sample, SamplePhoenix.Endpoint, http: [ip: {127, 0, 0, 1}, port: 5001], server: true, adapter: Bandit.PhoenixAdapter, secret_key_base: String.duplicate("a", 64) ) Mix.install([ {:bandit, "~> 1.8"}, {:phoenix, "~> 1.8"} ]) defmodule SamplePhoenix.SampleController do use Phoenix.Controller, formats: [:html, :json] use Phoenix.VerifiedRoutes, endpoint: SamplePhoenix.Endpoint, router: Router def index(conn, _) do send_resp(conn, 200, ~p"/ø") end end defmodule Router do use Phoenix.Router pipeline :browser do plug(:accepts, ["html"]) end scope "/", SamplePhoenix do pipe_through(:browser) get("/", SampleController, :index) get("/ø", SampleController, :index) # Prevent a horrible error because ErrorView is missing get("/favicon.ico", SampleController, :index) end end defmodule SamplePhoenix.Endpoint do use Phoenix.Endpoint, otp_app: :sample plug(Router) end {:ok, _} = Supervisor.start_link([SamplePhoenix.Endpoint], strategy: :one_for_one) Process.sleep(:infinity) ```
### Expected behavior That the route would be detected and work as a regular route would.
phoenixframework/phoenix#6524.title: `~p` sigil does not work with non-ascii characters

phoenixframework/phoenix#6585.body:
### Environment

* Elixir version (elixir -v): 1.19.0
* Phoenix version (mix deps): 1.8.3
* Operating system: Ubuntu 25.10

### Actual behavior
If a directory in the path has ":" within it, the "mix phx.new myapp" will die with an error. 

To my untrained eye in project.ex there seems to be an undocumented feature. If there's ":somevalue" in the path, it assumes we are wanting to replace the path with the value in project.somevalue. 

#### To reproduce do the following:
    mkdir 0:0
    cd 0:0
    mix phx.new test

      base_path: "/home/jeremy/0:0/test",
      app: "test",
      app_mod: Test,
      app_path: "/home/jeremy/0:0/test",

      root_app: "test",
      root_mod: Test,
      project_path: "/home/jeremy/0:0/test",
      web_app: "test",
      web_namespace: TestWeb,
      web_path: "/home/jeremy/0:0/test",
      opts: [],
      in_umbrella?: false,

        phoenix_dep_umbrella_root: "{:phoenix, \"~> 1.8.3\"}",
        phoenix_js_path: "phoenix",
        phoenix_version: %Version{major: 1, minor: 8, patch: 3},
        pubsub_server: Test.PubSub,
        secret_key_base_dev: "5KLpyO8rgQLPhylThGUIBIYkmrzprE0hcClHMG940D6vw/Zw0pYdctSNpzeQUcLE",
        secret_key_base_test: "FcOsw9Y+3EA6JHbXdGYRtCO6MW7sAiXzVyfk9p1p+ImiSVq602OfDlAzfsDohFJR",
        signing_salt: "zAFAUhXT",
        lv_signing_salt: "uLhpMMpX",
        in_umbrella: false,
        asset_builders: [:tailwind, :esbuild],
        javascript: true,
        css: true,
        mailer: true,

    (elixir 1.19.0) lib/enum.ex:2520: Enum."-reduce/3-lists^foldl/2-0-"/3
    lib/phx_new/generator.ex:72: Phx.New.Generator.copy_from/3

#### Extra Info
In my case, the default naming of an external drive trigger the error. I could also see someone who uses timestamps for directory names also running into this issue. 
/mnt/usb-Seagate_Expansion_HDD_00000000NT17GAFJ-0:0-part1/Dropbox/Code

Also, I was using a symlinked directory without the offending text when it happened.

### Expected behavior
It'd be nice if it handled this edge case without any changes from the end user. Second best might be to have a better error message, and update the documentation of phx.new.
phoenixframework/phoenix#6585.title: Having a colon in the path breaks phx.new.

phoenixframework/phoenix#6599.title: fix(socket): prevent WebSocket leak when multiple teardowns run

phoenixframework/phoenix#6612.body:
Bumps [@eslint/js](https://github.com/eslint/eslint/tree/HEAD/packages/js) from 9.39.2 to 10.0.1.
Release notes

Sourced from @​eslint/js's releases.

v10.0.1

Bug Fixes

  • c87d5bd fix: update eslint (#20531) (renovate[bot])
  • d841001 fix: update minimatch to 10.2.1 to address security vulnerabilities (#20519) (루밀LuMir)
  • 04c2147 fix: update error message for unused suppressions (#20496) (fnx)
  • 38b089c fix: update dependency @​eslint/config-array to ^0.23.1 (#20484) (renovate[bot])

Documentation

  • 5b3dbce docs: add AI acknowledgement section to templates (#20431) (루밀LuMir)
  • 6f23076 docs: toggle nav in no-JS mode (#20476) (Tanuj Kanti)
  • b69cfb3 docs: Update README (GitHub Actions Bot)

Chores

  • e5c281f chore: updates for v9.39.3 release (Jenkins)
  • 8c3832a chore: update @​typescript-eslint/parser to ^8.56.0 (#20514) (Milos Djermanovic)
  • 8330d23 test: add tests for config-api (#20493) (Milos Djermanovic)
  • 37d6e91 chore: remove eslint v10 prereleases from eslint-config-eslint deps (#20494) (Milos Djermanovic)
  • da7cd0e refactor: cleanup error message templates (#20479) (Francesco Trotta)
  • 84fb885 chore: package.json update for @​eslint/js release (Jenkins)
  • 1f66734 chore: add eslint to peerDependencies of @eslint/js (#20467) (Milos Djermanovic)

v10.0.0

Breaking Changes

  • f9e54f4 feat!: estimate rule-tester failure location (#20420) (ST-DDT)
  • a176319 feat!: replace chalk with styleText and add color to ResultsMeta (#20227) (루밀LuMir)
  • c7046e6 feat!: enable JSX reference tracking (#20152) (Pixel998)
  • fa31a60 feat!: add name to configs (#20015) (Kirk Waiblinger)
  • 3383e7e fix!: remove deprecated SourceCode methods (#20137) (Pixel998)
  • 501abd0 feat!: update dependency minimatch to v10 (#20246) (renovate[bot])
  • ca4d3b4 fix!: stricter rule tester assertions for valid test cases (#20125) (唯然)
  • 96512a6 fix!: Remove deprecated rule context methods (#20086) (Nicholas C. Zakas)
  • c69fdac feat!: remove eslintrc support (#20037) (Francesco Trotta)
  • 208b5cc feat!: Use ScopeManager#addGlobals() (#20132) (Milos Djermanovic)
  • a2ee188 fix!: add uniqueItems: true in no-invalid-regexp option (#20155) (Tanuj Kanti)
  • a89059d feat!: Program range span entire source text (#20133) (Pixel998)
  • 39a6424 fix!: assert 'text' is a string across all RuleFixer methods (#20082) (Pixel998)
  • f28fbf8 fix!: Deprecate "always" and "as-needed" options of the radix rule (#20223) (Milos Djermanovic)
  • aa3fb2b fix!: tighten func-names schema (#20119) (Pixel998)
  • f6c0ed0 feat!: report eslint-env comments as errors (#20128) (Francesco Trotta)
  • 4bf739f fix!: remove deprecated LintMessage#nodeType and TestCaseError#type (#20096) (Pixel998)
  • 523c076 feat!: drop support for jiti < 2.2.0 (#20016) (michael faith)
  • 454a292 feat!: update eslint:recommended configuration (#20210) (Pixel998)
  • 4f880ee feat!: remove v10_* and inactive unstable_* flags (#20225) (sethamus)
  • f18115c feat!: no-shadow-restricted-names report globalThis by default (#20027) (sethamus)
  • c6358c3 feat!: Require Node.js ^20.19.0 || ^22.13.0 || >=24 (#20160) (Milos Djermanovic)

Features

  • bff9091 feat: handle Array.fromAsync in array-callback-return (#20457) (Francesco Trotta)
  • 290c594 feat: add self to no-implied-eval rule (#20468) (sethamus)
  • 43677de feat: fix handling of function and class expression names in no-shadow (#20432) (Milos Djermanovic)

... (truncated)

Commits

  • 1f66734 chore: add eslint to peerDependencies of @eslint/js (#20467)
  • f3fbc2f chore: set @eslint/js version to 10.0.0 to skip releasing it (#20466)
  • b4b3127 chore: package.json update for @​eslint/js release
  • 0b14059 chore: package.json update for @​eslint/js release

phoenixframework/phoenix#6620.body:
### Environment

* Elixir version (elixir -v): Elixir 1.18.3 (compiled with Erlang/OTP 27)
* Phoenix version (mix deps): phoenix 1.8.4
* Operating system: MacOS

### Actual behavior

Currently phoenix socket always connects after leaving and returning to page. It doesn't wait for manual connect.

Steps to reproduce:
1. Create new phoenix project.
2. In assets js put creating new Socket

```js
import "phoenix_html";
import { Socket } from "phoenix";

let csrfToken = document
  .querySelector("meta[name='csrf-token']")
  .getAttribute("content");

window.socket = new Socket("/live", {
  params: { _csrf_token: csrfToken },
});
```

3. Change browser tab and return
4. Check connection state

```js
socket.isConnected()
```

### Expected behaviour

1. Socket should wait for calling `connect`. 
2. Socket shouldn't reconnect when manually disconnected


### Proposed change: 

Setting `closeWasClean` to true in the constructor of `Socket` should fix the thing.

### Additional Info

Related issue:
#6578 

Related PR:
#6579
phoenixframework/phoenix#6620.title: Socket connects automatically on page visibility change

phoenixframework/phoenix#6624.body:
Hi,

I was confused by one of the `flash` component usage examples: 

```elixir
 <.flash kind={:info} phx-mounted={show("#flash")}>Welcome Back!</.flash
```

Location: https://github.com/phoenixframework/phoenix/blob/850162d618cf4e31be82fb53ad6e259095fa6126/installer/templates/phx_web/components/core_components.ex#L40


I assume that `phx-mounted` is there to make the flash show up on load. If that's the correct assumption, then I can see two issues with it: 
- the flash still appears even without `phx-mounted={show("#flash")`, because of `:if={msg = render_slot(@inner_block) || Phoenix.Flash.get(@flash, @kind)}` and the classes don't contain `hidden` - ` class="toast toast-top toast-end z-50"` 
- the default id includes flash kind: ` assigns = assign_new(assigns, :id, fn -> "flash-#{assigns.kind}" end)`, so  `show("#flash")` won't match `#flash-info`

Can you please advise what am I missing?
phoenixframework/phoenix#6624.title: Should the flash example be corrected?

phoenixframework/phoenix_live_view#617.body:
### Environment

* Elixir version (elixir -v): 1.10
* Phoenix version (mix deps): 1.4.12
* Phoenix LiveView version (mix deps): 0.6.0
* NodeJS version (node -v):
* NPM version (npm -v):
* Operating system:

### Feature request

Normal we can disable socket logging with `use Phoenix.Socket, log: false` but this is not possible with live_view since this is internal to the library. Exposing this option would helpful for those that do custom logging.
phoenixframework/phoenix_live_view#617.title: Add option to disable socket_connected logging

phoenixframework/phoenix_live_view#3896.body: ### Environment * Elixir version (elixir -v): `1.18.4-erlang-27.3.4.1` * Phoenix version (mix deps): `1.7.21` (also confirmed on `1.8.0-rc.3`) * Phoenix LiveView version (mix deps): `1.0.7` (also confirmed on `1.0.17`) * Operating system: `debian-bullseye-20250630-slim` * Browsers you attempted to reproduce this bug on (the more the merrier): Safari on iPhone * Does the problem persist after removing "assets/node_modules" and trying again? Yes/no: yes ### Actual behavior On a freshly created liveview app, when reopening a liveview tab on mobile after a while (switching to tabs/apps in btw or locking/unlocking), a full reload is triggered, losing client state and flashing an error message. I managed to consistently reproduce it on an iPhone with the following steps (unlocking is more predictable than switching tabs): - open the tab - lock the phone with the side button - wait ~10s - unlock the phone This issue is being discussed on https://elixirforum.com/t/mobile-tab-switching-triggers-full-page-reload-in-liveview-is-this-expected-behavior/71188. As far as I can tell, older versions of liveview (at least [livebeats](https://livebeats.fly.dev/signin), released a [year ago](https://github.com/fly-apps/live_beats/tree/master)?) don't seem impacted. ### Expected behavior No full-reload, no error message.
phoenixframework/phoenix_live_view#3896.title: Mobile Tab switching triggers full page reload and error flash
